title_en: True Patriots Are Cashing In on the Apocalypse
title_zh: 真正的“爱国者”正在从末日叙事中赚取利润

paragraphs.en: When it comes to prepping, look to the Mormons. It’s right there, in the official name of the religion: To be a “Latter-day Saint”' is explicitly to believe in, and prepare for, the end times. This is why, on a calm morning last September, I arrive just outside Salt Lake City in a place called American Fork and knock on the door of Tyler Stapleton, the chief product engineer for off-grid power products at 4Patriots, one of the biggest companies pushing preparedness into the mainstream. Church members play outsize roles in this multibillion-dollar industry—a global concern where politics and technology collide, with sales fueled by social media and conspiracy theories, in addition to the undeniable rise in world chaos.
paragraphs.zh: 说到应急储备，不妨看看摩门教徒。连宗教的正式名称都写明了这一点：“后期圣徒”就是明确要相信并为末世到来做准备的人。这就是为什么去年9月一个风平浪静的早晨，我来到犹他州盐湖城外的美国福克镇，走到4Patriots的首席离网电力产品工程师Tyler Stapleton家门前敲门。4Patriots是将“做好准备”推向主流的最大公司之一。教会成员在这个价值数十亿美元的行业中扮演着举足轻重的角色——这是一个政治与技术交织的全球议题，社交媒体和阴谋论为销售提供了助推，再加上世界局势日益混乱这一不争事实。

paragraphs.en: Stapleton is a bit sheepish but very nice, a nerdy mechanical engineer with degrees from Brigham Young University who lives with his wife and three young children. Inside their tidy home is a big clock, a bright kitchen, and the Book of Mormon on the shelf. After exchanging pleasantries, Stapleton begins showing off new products and prototypes, including a sleek “James Bond-like” solar generator that packs up like a briefcase. All the while, he sticks to the company line, telling me that the typical owner of a 4Patriots power product is not some zealot in camouflage preparing to live in a bunker “for days on end” but rather a family man seeking “energy independence” for the occasional moments when the grid goes down. It’s a well-placed piece of rhetoric, though the line loses some of its luster an hour later, when Tyler lets slip that his childhood home nearby does, indeed, feature a modest bunker, then reluctantly agrees to take us to it.
paragraphs.zh: Stapleton有些腼腆但非常和蔼，他是个有点“书呆子气”的机械工程师，毕业于杨百翰大学，与妻子和三个年幼孩子住在一起。他家的屋内很整洁：有一只大钟、明亮的厨房，以及架上的《摩门经》。寒暄过后，Stapleton开始向我展示新产品和原型，包括一款“像詹姆斯·邦德风格”的太阳能发电机，收纳后像公文包一样小巧。全程他都坚持公司的表述：4Patriots电力产品的典型购买者并不是穿着迷彩、打算在地堡里“连续待上好几天”的狂热分子，而更像是普通的家庭主，而是在偶发停电时追求“能源独立”的人。这套说辞听上去很恰当，但一小时后一点一点被削弱：Tyler不经意间提到，他附近的童年故居确实有一个不大的地堡，随后也勉强同意带我们去看。

paragraphs.en: Today, the US government advises every citizen to build a disaster kit and concoct plans for an array of cataclysms. According to a 2023 survey from the Federal Emergency Management Agency, 51 percent of Americans are in some way “prepared for a disaster.” This helps explain why the prepping industry’s market cap is forecast to be nearly $300 billion by the end of the decade. Two of the firms competing for market share have nearly identical names and offer the same basic slate of products: power generators, dehydrated food, and water filtration. There’s Stapleton’s employer, 4Patriots, and its rival, My Patriot Supply. While exact revenue figures are difficult to pin down, officials at My Patriot Supply tell me that, before the pandemic, they rented part of a 45,000-square-foot space; today, the company built and fully utilizes a 428,000-square-foot warehouse not far from Stapleton’s home.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，美国政府建议每位公民都要组建灾难应急包，并为各种灾难场景制定预案。根据联邦紧急事务管理署（FEMA）2023年的一项调查，51%的美国人在某种程度上“已做好应灾准备”。这也能解释为何业内市场规模预计在本十年末接近3000亿美元。两家争夺市场份额的公司名字几乎一模一样，出售的核心产品也高度重合：发电机、脱水食品和净水设备。它们是Stapleton任职的4Patriots，以及竞争对手My Patriot Supply。虽然精确营收数字难以核实，但My Patriot Supply的一位负责人告诉我，在疫情前他们只租用了4.5万平方英尺的仓储空间；如今，公司在离Stapleton家不远处建成并充分使用了42.8万平方英尺的仓库。

paragraphs.en: These two firms are aggressively jockeying to be the industry’s true patriot prepping company, with one official from My Patriot Supply claiming that 4Patriots is their “archnemesis.” My Patriot Supply is particularly bellicose, deeming the other company and its competitors “fake patriots”in a video. Meanwhile, both firms have notched their share of consumer complaints with government and business watchdogs over the years. 4Patriots customers have alleged that the company produces faulty water filters, defective generators, and dubious food. One person said their order was rife with roaches, while another claimed the company's Aztec Chili with Mango made them “can’t leave the bathroom sick” for a week. (According to the Better Business Bureau website, someone associated with 4Patriots replied to this alleged complaint a day later, adding, "I'm so sorry that you had many issues after consuming the survival food kits.”) In 2016, the company voluntarily recalled a couple thousand generators due to an unforeseen fire hazard, and consumer complaints allege their generators continue to suffer from potentially dangerous electrical defects. (After putting 4Patriots’ 1,800-watt solar generator through lab testing, Consumer Reports rated it 46 out of 100.) Certainly, recalls and bad reviews are part and parcel of many businesses today, though an explicit part of the pitch from 4Patriots and its competitors is the rock-hard reliability of their goods when disaster strikes.
paragraphs.zh: 这两家公司都在激烈争夺“真正的爱国者应急公司”这一行业头衔。My Patriot Supply的一名官员甚至称4Patriots是他们的“头号宿敌”。在一段视频中，My Patriot Supply尤其好斗，称另一家公司及其竞争者是“伪爱国者”。与此同时，多年来双方都在政府和商贸监管机构那里留下了不少消费者投诉记录。4Patriots的客户指控该公司生产了有缺陷的净水器、故障率高的发电机和可疑食品。有人说他们的订单里爬满了蟑螂；另有人称公司出售的“芒果味阿兹特克辣酱”（Aztec Chili with Mango）让他“生病到一周都离不开浴室”。（根据“更好商业局”网站资料，有人代表4Patriots在次日回应了这条被指控的投诉，写道：“I’m so sorry that you had many issues after consuming the survival food kits.”）2016年，公司主动召回了数千台发电机，原因是一个未预料到的起火隐患，而消费者投诉还称其发电机仍持续存在潜在危险的电气缺陷。（在将4Patriots的1800瓦太阳能发电机送往实验室测试后，消费者报告将其评为46分（满分100分）。）当然，召回和差评在当今很多企业都很常见；但对4Patriots及其竞争对手来说，它们的营销话术却明确把“灾难来临时产品坚如磐石般可靠”作为核心卖点。

paragraphs.en: In a comment to WIRED, a 4Patriots official said the company shipped over 5 million products to consumers over the past three years and that it places “product quality and customer safety above all else.” He declined to detail the company’s return or refund rates, asserting that to equate those numbers with “product failure or customer dissatisfaction would be inaccurate.”
paragraphs.zh: 一位4Patriots的官员在接受WIRED评论时表示，公司在过去三年向消费者出货了超过500万件产品，并表示公司将“产品质量和客户安全放在第一位”。他拒绝详细披露公司的退货或退款率，并称把这些数字直接等同于“产品故障或客户不满意”是不准确的。

paragraphs.en: Video: Sinna Nasseri
paragraphs.zh: 视频：Sinna Nasseri

paragraphs.en: Stapleton, for his part, is bullish about the current generation of 4Patriots’ generators. We end his house tour in the kitchen, where he volunteers to demonstrate the company’s AlphaCase suitcase-sized generator, a product that is “powerful enough,” he says, to run his refrigerator. As he shimmies it out from a kitchen wall, Stapleton talks excitedly about the incredible advances in battery technology pioneered in recent years by firms like Tesla. “It’s competitive, and that’s a good thing,” Stapleton says. “There’s a lot of people pushing the envelope on what’s possible. And so we are working hard to stay ahead of that.”
paragraphs.zh: 就斯台普尔顿本人而言，他对当前一代4Patriots发电机非常看好。我们在他的厨房结束了房屋参观，在那里他主动演示了公司推出的AlphaCase手提箱尺寸发电机，这款产品他称其“足够强大”，能够为冰箱供电。当他把它从厨房墙壁边缓缓取下时，斯台普尔顿兴奋地谈到近几年像特斯拉这样的公司在电池技术方面带来的惊人进步。“这是有竞争力的，而且这是件好事，”斯台普尔顿说，“有很多人正在突破可行性的边界。因此我们也在努力保持领先。”

paragraphs.en: As the climactic moment arrives, Stapleton unplugs his fridge and hooks it up to the 4Patriots power pack. It buzzes quietly for a few seconds, then emits a beeping sound. At this point, a message shoots up on the screen. “That’s just the overload message,” Stapleton awkwardly explains, adding that “this guy”—meaning his generator, which he had just said would run a refrigerator and which the website claims, too—is “a little bit small for powering fridges.”
paragraphs.zh: 在最关键的一刻到来时，斯台普尔顿拔下冰箱插头并将其接到4Patriots电源组合上。它静静嗡嗡作响了几秒，然后发出蜂鸣声。就在这时，屏幕上跳出一条消息。“这只是过载提示，”斯台普尔顿有些尴尬地解释道，并补充说，“这家伙”——也就是他刚才说能驱动冰箱、网站也声称能够驱动冰箱的那台发电机——“有点太小，不太适合给冰箱供电。”

paragraphs.en: The garden at Stapleton’s father’s home.
paragraphs.zh: 斯塔普尔顿父亲家的花园。

paragraphs.en: When, in the mid-1800s, the Mormons first fled west to the Utah Territory, they viewed prepping as a means of survival and autonomy from government persecution. They subsequently formed an off-grid society, with the church operating its own bank, printing its own currency, and directing a militia. In an October 1980 speech before thousands in Salt Lake City, church leader Ezra Taft Benson articulated a far more practical ethic of preparedness. A well-studied farmer, Benson spearheaded the reintroduction of missionary work in Europe following World War II. In his speech, Benson recalled the “terrible physical and social side effects of hunger” he witnessed in the wake of the war, his voice breaking with emotion. “Too often,” he said, “we bask in our comfortable complacency and rationalize that the ravages of war, economic disaster, famine, and earthquake cannot happen here.”
paragraphs.zh: 在19世纪中叶，摩门教徒最初向西迁往犹他领地时，他们把储备意识视为生存和摆脱政府迫害控制的手段。此后，他们建立了一个“脱离电网”的社会，教会运营自己的银行，印制自己的货币，并指挥民兵。在1980年10月于盐湖城对数千人发表的演讲中，教会领袖以斯拉·泰夫特·本森（Ezra Taft Benson）阐明了一套更务实的备灾伦理。作为一位研究极深的农民，本森在二战后率先重新启动了欧洲的传教工作。在演讲中，他回忆起自己在战后目睹到的“战争带来的可怕的身体和社会后果”，说到这里语带哽咽：“我们太常会沉浸在舒适的自满中，并为自己开脱，理所当然地认为战争、经济灾难、饥荒和地震这些破坏不会在这里发生。”

paragraphs.en: Mormons, of course, were not the only group of Americans to worry about the future; Boy Scouts who hope to become Eagles have long needed to earn an Emergency Preparedness Merit Badge. In their 2024 book, Be Prepared: Doomsday Prepping in the United States, Robert E. Kirsch and Emily Ray trace how American prepping has long been wed to consumerism through its alluring pitch to “purchase your way to safety.” Today, America’s roulette wheel of routine calamity includes extreme heat, power grid overload, crop failure, water contamination, climate-change-induced natural disasters, climate-change-induced disease, government shutdowns, political corruption, troops in the streets, fervent militia activity, mass shootings—and, new for 2026, regime change in Latin America. It also seems that every few years an asteroid just barely misses Earth.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，摩门教徒并不是唯一担忧未来的美国群体；许多希望成为鹰派的童子军，多年来一直需要获得“应急准备”勋章。罗伯特·E·柯克什（Robert E. Kirsch）和艾米莉·雷（Emily Ray）在2024年的《Be Prepared: Doomsday Prepping in the United States》（《做好准备：美国的世界末日防备》）一书中，追溯了美国“防备”文化如何长期与消费主义捆绑在一起，因为其吸引人的口号是“用购买来换取安全”。如今，美国这场日常灾难的轮盘赌中包括极端高温、输电网过载、作物减产、水体污染、气候变化引发的自然灾害、气候变化引发的疾病、政府停摆、政治腐败、街头军队、狂热的民兵活动、大规模枪击案，以及——2026年的新增风险——拉丁美洲的政权更迭。似乎每隔几年，就会有一颗小行星几乎擦过地球。

paragraphs.en: Final Famine, a book by Teddy Daniels stocked at the My Patriot Supply warehouse in Salt Lake City.
paragraphs.zh: 塞迪·丹尼尔斯（Teddy Daniels）的《Final Famine》一书陈列在盐湖城的 My Patriot Supply 仓库里。

paragraphs.en: Founded in 2008, 4Patriots initially focused on food, appealing to people who started stocking emergency rations after the election of Barack Obama. Its unlikely founder is Allen Baler, who has an English degree from Obama’s law school alma mater, Harvard, and a corporate marketing background, traits that My Patriot Supply has spoofed in YouTube content showing a Baler-esque figure in a Harvard half-zip who resorts to “scarcity” tactics and other cynical sales tricks.
paragraphs.zh: 4Patriots 成立于2008年，最初专注于食品销售，吸引了那些在巴拉克·奥巴马当选后开始囤积应急口粮的人群。其并不常见的创始人是艾伦·贝勒（Allen Baler），他拥有奥巴马法学院母校哈佛大学的英语学位，并有企业营销背景，这些特征也被 My Patriot Supply 在 YouTube 的内容中戏仿：视频里出现了一个类似贝勒的角色，穿着哈佛校牌半拉链衫，动用“稀缺性”策略及其他带有讽刺意味的销售手法。

paragraphs.en: The face of the firm back then was Frank Bates, a slick pitchman and Vietnam veteran living near Nashville, Tennessee, who wrote about how Obama's policies had hurt his family, his business, and America. He alleged various government plots, including one that the FEMA was hoarding food because “it knows that if you control the food supply, then you control the people.” When the company’s emergency offerings expanded into power supplies, 4Patriots teased the “dirty little secret that President Obama and the big energy monopolies have been trying to bury.”
paragraphs.zh: 当时公司代表人物是弗兰克·贝茨（Frank Bates），他是位讲得很会“忽悠”、住在田纳西州纳什维尔附近的越战老兵，在文稿中写道奥巴马的政策伤害了他的家庭、他的生意和美国。他指控政府有各种阴谋，包括联邦紧急事务管理局（FEMA）在囤积食物，因为“它知道如果你控制了粮食供应，就能控制人民”。当该公司的应急产品线扩展到电源设备时，4Patriots 又抛出“奥巴马总统和大型能源垄断企业一直试图掩盖的肮脏小秘密”。

paragraphs.en: 4Patriots once described its core customer base as “55+ year-old conservatives in ‘red’ states with a strong sense of self-reliance.” Baler first tapped into this constituency in 2011, with an ad buy at a then-fledgling conservative news outlet called Newsmax, a hub, in his words, of “affluent, conservative men who surprisingly like to buy a lot of stuff online.” He subsequently expanded his messaging to The National Review, Glenn Beck’s conservative news network, The Blaze, and then Fox News, which has repeatedly plugged 4Patriots products on air.
paragraphs.zh: 4Patriots 曾将其核心客户群描述为“年龄在55岁以上、居住在‘红色’州、且有很强自力更生意识的保守派”。贝勒在2011年首次瞄准了这类人群，在当时还在起步阶段的保守媒体 Newsmax 投放广告。用他的话说，这里是“富裕的保守男性聚集地，出乎意料的是，他们很喜欢在网上购买大量商品”。随后他把宣传内容扩展到了《国家评论》（The National Review）、格伦·贝克（Glenn Beck）的保守新闻网络 The Blaze，接着到福克斯新闻（Fox News），后者多次在节目中推介4Patriots的产品。

paragraphs.en: In 2014, ThinkProgress alleged that Bates wasn’t real. They could find no one in Nashville with his name and background. Then the site discovered his likeness on a stock image site, captioned “friendly man with arms crossed.” 4Patriots denied any deception and, to this day, occasionally features materials signed by the mysterious Mr. Bates. (On its FAQ page, 4Patriots assures longtime customers that Bates is, indeed, real but that he uses a pen name “to protect his privacy.”) In general, 4Patriots has toned things down, presumably to reach other constituencies, like crunchy off-gridders, tailgaters, campers, and people living in hurricane zones or tornado alley. Still, according to Stapleton, the firm’s customer base continues to include “a lot of conservative individuals.”
paragraphs.zh: 2014年，ThinkProgress 指控贝茨并不真实。该媒体在纳什维尔找不到任何与其姓名和经历相符的人。后来他们在一个素材站点上发现了他的肖像，标题写着“笑脸交叉双臂的男性”。4Patriots 否认有任何欺骗行为，而且到今天仍偶尔刊登署名为神秘的贝茨先生的材料。（在其 FAQ 页面上，4Patriots 向老顾客保证贝茨“确实是真实存在的”，但他使用笔名“是为了保护隐私”。）总体而言，4Patriots 已经把宣传语气淡化了，推测是为了吸引其他群体，例如注重脱网自给生活的“极客”、赛前集体观赛和烧烤人群、露营者，以及住在飓风高发区或“龙卷风走廊”地区的人群。不过，按照 Stapleton 的说法，该公司现有客户群仍然包含“相当多的保守派个人”。

paragraphs.en: Stapleton in his garage in American Fork, Utah, near Salt Lake City.
paragraphs.zh: 斯塔普尔顿站在他位于犹他州美国福克（靠近盐湖城）的车库里。

paragraphs.en: Both of the patriotic prepping companies promote their products through traditional TV ads and active YouTube channels. My Patriot Supply also produces podcast-style content about the various dangers that necessitate a prepping mindset, while its competitor has launched 4Patriots University, a set of preparedness master classes taught weekly by Seth Weller, a wiry former Boy Scout (and scoutmaster), who has been working in preparedness for a quarter-century. Weller insists that his curriculum focuses strictly on developing contingencies and building community support networks, not pushing product. He argues that too many Americans use prepping as a form of “retail therapy,” buying products “to remove that fear” without building the requisite expertise to actually use them.
paragraphs.zh: 这两家“爱国”预备公司都通过传统电视广告和活跃的 YouTube 频道来推广其产品。My Patriot Supply 还制作播客式内容，讲述各种使人需要预备心态的风险；而其竞争对手则推出了 4Patriots University，由瘦削的前男童军（也是童军领队）Seth Weller 每周讲授一套“生存准备”核心课程。Weller 反复强调，他的课程重心在于严格构建应对预案并建立社区支持网络，而不是推销产品。他认为，太多美国人把“预备”当成一种“购物疗法”，购买东西只是为了“减轻恐惧”，却没有建立真正能够在关键时刻使用这些东西的必备能力。

paragraphs.en: Weller’s long career on the front lines of American prepping has made him a canny diagnostician of the emotional currents fueling the phenomenon. In his estimation, many of his clients have experienced some sort of profound loss, whether it’s heartache, the death of a loved one, or a storm. “It’s not necessarily some major catastrophic event, but it’s something that changed their lives,” he tells me. “And in that process, they realized that this happened because, well, they had a dependency. And then they turn around, like, ‘I don’t ever want to feel that again.’ So, they realize they have to gain some control. They’ve got to gain independence.”
paragraphs.zh: Weller 在美国“生存预备”一线工作的长久经历，使他成为一种驱动这一现象的情绪潮流的敏锐观察者。依他的估计，许多客户都经历过某种深重的失落，不管是情感创伤、亲人去世，还是一次风暴。“这不一定是某个重大的灾难性事件，而可能是改变了他们生活的某件事，”他对我说，“在这个过程中，他们意识到之所以会发生，是因为……他们有一种依赖。然后他们会反过来想：‘我再也不想有那种感觉了。’所以，他们意识到自己必须拿回一些控制权。他们必须获得独立。”

paragraphs.en: When I ask Weller what thrust him into this world, he points not to an earthquake or a flood but to the painful and unexpected loss of his luxury port-a-potty and shower trailer business. He seems genuinely driven to help people secure calm from their anxieties, arguing that it’s interpersonal prepping networks and skills that can best meet this challenge, not simply gear from prepping companies, which, Weller candidly admits, doesn’t always work.
paragraphs.zh: 当我问 Weller 是什么把他推向这个领域时，他指向的不是地震或洪水，而是他那家高端移动厕所和淋浴车厢生意的突如其来又令人痛苦的倒闭。他似乎真心想帮助人们从焦虑中找到平静，认为最能应对这类问题的是人际间的预备网络和技能，而不是仅靠预备公司出售的装备——Weller 也坦率承认，这些装备并不总是管用。

paragraphs.en: Unprompted, Weller invokes a hypothetically wonky generator, bought “under the guise” that it can power a critical household appliance, only for the lights to go out and the consumer to realize that the product “doesn’t work.” Obviously unaware of my prior experience in Stapleton’s kitchen, Weller claims that 4Patriots has risen above this shoddy consumerism. “It’s not just about selling products for us,” he assures me. “It’s about truly helping them out.”
paragraphs.zh: Weller未等我开口，就提到一个假设中质量很差的发电机——那东西“打着”能给关键家用电器供电的幌子被卖给消费者，结果一熄灯，买家就发现产品“根本不能用”。他显然不知道我在Stapleton厨房里的先前经历，Weller声称4Patriots已经超越了这种粗制滥造的消费主义。他向我保证：这对他们来说不只是卖产品，而是“真正帮助消费者”。

paragraphs.en: Fire starter sold by My Patriot Supply at the company’s warehouse in Salt Lake City.
paragraphs.zh: My Patriot Supply在盐湖城公司仓库出售的打火工具。

paragraphs.en: One of Weller’s longtime protégés is Gary Eiler, an Air Force veteran who, in the fall of 2018, was caught in the vortex of Hurricane Michael. At the time, Eiler owned an 800-unit climate-controlled storage facility in Panama City, Florida, called HBO Storage. (It stood for Home, Business, Office.) While Michael was initially predicted to make landfall in Louisiana, it hooked north at the last minute, rapidly intensified to a Category 5, and barreled directly toward Panama City where Eiler was standing watch. As winds topped out at 160 miles per hour, the storm stripped the roof off Eiler’s business like a sardine can. Then the walls started collapsing. Eiler found cover under a bolted-down service counter, where he huddled for more than six hours. “It was very emotionally trying,” he confesses.
paragraphs.zh: Weller的一位长期门徒是加里·艾勒（Gary Eiler），一位空军退伍军人。2018年秋天，他在飓风迈克尔（Hurricane Michael）中遭遇了旋涡般的袭击。当时，艾勒在佛罗里达州巴拿马城经营着一家拥有800个单元、带有温控系统的仓储设施，名为HBO Storage（代表Home, Business, Office）。当时迈克尔最初被预测会在路易斯安那州登陆，最后在最后时刻突然向北转向，迅速增强为五级飓风，并直接迎向艾勒守望的巴拿马城。风速最高达到每小时160英里，风暴把艾勒的店铺屋顶像打开沙丁鱼罐头一样撕走；随后墙体开始倒塌。艾勒躲到一个被螺栓固定的服务台下，在那里蜷缩了超过六个小时。“这非常让人精神上承受不住，”他承认。

paragraphs.en: As the wind settled, Eiler became fixated on a question Weller had posed in a class he taught prior to his 4Patriots employment: “How long do you have in an emergency before societal breakdown ensues?” Eiler says Panama City turned to bedlam in about 20 minutes after the wind died down. Hordes of citizens began looting the stores around him, including a Goodyear auto shop, an off-brand rent-a-center, and a grocery store called Food City. Eiler went to sleep that night in the collapsed ruins of his business, the sounds of screams and gunfire punctuating the air. (News reports show that the storm indeed inflicted massive infrastructure damage and took the lives of 19 people. There were also sustained reports of armed looters, but also waves of fear that crashed across communities, leading one Florida state cop to shoot and kill a civilian who allegedly tried to steal a law enforcement vehicle.)
paragraphs.zh: 风势稍停后，艾勒越来越执着于Weller在他加入4Patriots之前的一次课堂上提出的问题：‘在紧急情况下，社会崩解前你们通常能撑多久？’艾勒说，随着风停下来，巴拿马城大约在二十分钟后就变成了乱象之地。人群蜂拥而至，开始抢劫周围的商店，包括一家Goodyear汽车维修店、一家非主流的Rent-A-Center商家，以及一家名叫Food City的杂货店。那天夜里，艾勒在自己公司倒塌后的废墟中入睡，周围空气中不断被尖叫声和枪声打断。（新闻报道称，这场风暴确实造成了大规模基础设施破坏，并夺走了19条生命。也有持续报道称有携带武器的掠夺者出没；同时，恐慌浪潮席卷社区，导致一名佛罗里达州警官开枪击毙了一名据称试图偷窃执法车辆的平民。）

paragraphs.en: Eiler spent two months stationed at his destroyed store, overseeing his customers’ belongings with a stern face and a Taurus pistol. In the first two weeks, he collected and filtered water off the section of the roof that was still intact and prepared freeze-dried food, remembering Weller’s specific instructions to cook it in such a way as to avoid constipation, which, Eiler remarks, “you don’t want to deal with in an environment like the one I was in.” Indeed, he was then defecating in a bucket. Eventually his brother was able to breach the city in an RV filled with food, clothes, more weapons, and other survival gear supplied by Weller and friends. “It was a war zone,” Eiler concludes. “I’ve only been to Panama City two other times since the storm because of the anxiety it brings me. And that’s because my sister lives here.”
paragraphs.zh: 埃勒在被摧毁的商店里驻守了两个月，面带严肃神情、配着一把塔罗斯手枪，照看着顾客们留存的财物。前两周，他从仍然完好的屋顶区域收集并净化水，准备脱水冻干食物，并牢记韦勒的具体指示：要用他指定的方式烹煮，以免便秘。埃勒说：“在我当时那种环境里，你可不想处理便秘的问题。”确实，他当时是在一个桶里上厕所。后来，他的兄弟终于在一辆满载食物、衣物、更多武器和其他生存装备的房车中闯入城市，这些物资都由韦勒和朋友们提供。“那是个战区，”埃勒总结道。“自那场风暴后，我只又去过巴拿马城两次，因为它带给我的焦虑太重了。原因也很清楚，因为我妹妹住在这里。”

paragraphs.en: Prepared dishes from 4Patriots survival food kit.
paragraphs.zh: 来自4Patriots生存食品套件的预制菜肴。

paragraphs.en: Another of Weller’s star students at 4Patriots University is Bill Knapp, whose worries, unlike Eiler’s, were crystalized by an event that never happened. In 1998, Knapp was at Deloitte, where he helped the firm prepare for Y2K, work that first exposed him to the grid’s fragility. Ahead of New Year’s Eve, Knapp stocked up on water and food and bought a generator. Nothing happened, though during one summer shortly after, he says, a hurricane came through his community on Long Island and knocked out power for 10 days.
paragraphs.zh: 韦勒大学的另一位明星学生是比尔·克纳普，他的担忧与埃勒不同，是由一件从未发生的事件转化为近乎“具象化”的忧虑。1998年，克纳普在德勤任职，协助公司为“千禧年问题（Y2K）”做准备，这段经历最早让他看见了电网的脆弱。跨年夜前，克纳普囤积了水和食物，并买了一台发电机。结果什么也没发生，但他后来提到，不久后的一个夏天，长岛地区一场飓风曾来袭并导致全社区停电长达10天。

paragraphs.en: Knapp connected with 4Patriots three and a half years ago when, during a routine Google search for a cell phone charger, he found the company’s solar-powered block. Knapp today estimates that he has spent roughly $60,000 on prepping, much of it to support the construction of an off-the-grid tiny house, but also more than $7,000 alone in 4Patriots gear. He grouses that people “accuse you of hoarding, but I'm not hoarding. I’m investing.”
paragraphs.zh: 三年半前，克纳普在一次例行谷歌搜索手机充电器时，偶然找到4Patriots的官网太阳能充电模块，于是与其建立了联系。如今他估计自己在“准备”上共花了大约6万美元，其中大部分用于建设离网小屋，但仅在4Patriots装备上的花费就超过7000美元。他抱怨道：人们“指责你囤积，但我不是囤积，我是在投资。”

paragraphs.en: Along the way, Knapp brought his new wife, Pat, into the fold. One of his first gifts to her when they were dating included a 4Patriots emergency kit for her car trunk, replete with thermal blankets and food should any chaos arise on I-95. When the two tied the knot last summer, they gifted some of their guests 4Patriots’ small device chargers. The couple has since converted the front lawn of their Maryland home to a flower garden, to support pollination, and grow food out back that doesn’t touch pesticides. After they discovered that their city water supply had allegedly been polluted with forever chemicals by the company that produces Gore-Tex fabric, they bought a new water filtration system.
paragraphs.zh: 在这个过程中，克纳普把他的新婚妻子帕特也带进了这个圈子。二人约会时，他送给她的第一批礼物之一，是一个放在她汽车后备箱里的 4Patriots 应急套装，里面装着保暖毯和食物，以防在95号州际公路上出现混乱。去年夏天这对夫妇完婚时，他们还给一些来宾送了4Patriots的小型设备充电器。此后，这对夫妇把他们在马里兰州的房子前草坪改成了花园，用于支持传粉，并在后院种植不使用杀虫剂的食物。后来他们发现自己的市政供水据称被生产 Gore-Tex 面料的公司污染了“永久化学物质”，于是买了新的净水系统。

paragraphs.en: Video: Sinna Nasseri
paragraphs.zh: 视频：Sinna Nasseri

paragraphs.en: Despite all this, Pat seems slightly more tentative of prepping than her husband. More specifically, she doesn’t love the constant vigilance that prepping culture can require. “Because of 4Patriots, whenever we go anywhere, we’re assessing,” she tells me, articulating the newfound hyperalertness that has suffused her life. “If it was not for 4Patriots, I would not be thinking this way. What are we taking? Are we going to a crowd? Are we staying away from a crowd? It’s dangerous to be out on the road, it really is. But we try to enjoy our lives. We try to balance—and we talk about this—how to balance out our negative vibes.”
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，帕特似乎对“预备”这一做法比丈夫更谨慎。更具体地说，她不喜欢“自给自足文化”可能带来的持续高度警觉。“有了4Patriots，不管去哪里我们都会在做评估。”她对我说，讲出了这股近来充斥她生活的警觉感。“如果不是4Patriots，我就不会这样思考：我们要带什么？我们要去人多的地方吗？还是躲开人多的地方？在路上真的很危险，确实如此。但我们还是尽量享受生活。我们也尽量做平衡——我们会谈一谈——怎么把这些负面情绪平衡掉。”

paragraphs.en: Pat expresses especially acute concern about the have and have-not dynamics that pervade any major disaster. “I’m not so sure I’m gonna be strong enough to turn somebody away if they need food.” She continues: “I’ve just started learning how to shoot. And I’m thinking, am I gonna be able to pull a gun out and shoot somebody because they’re trying to … ”
paragraphs.zh: 帕特尤其担心在任何重大灾难中都会出现的“有的人有得、没的人没得”的局面。“我不太确定自己会不会有足够的力量把人拒之门外——如果他们饿了、需要食物的话。”她接着说：“我才刚开始学射击。我在想，我会不会在这种情况下把枪拿出来，朝有人——因为他们在试图……”

paragraphs.en: “Steal from us,” Bill finishes.
paragraphs.zh: “抢我们吧，”Bill 说完。

paragraphs.en: “ … steal from us,” Pat echoes. “Or hurt Bill. These are the types of things we discuss in the chat rooms.”
paragraphs.zh: “…抢我们吧，”Pat 复述道。“要么伤害 Bill。这些就是我们在聊天群里讨论的内容。”

paragraphs.en: The threat of atomic annihilation was the catalyzing fear of the boomer prepper, while many younger ones point to 9/11. Other major events in modern prepping lore include Hurricane Katrina, the Ebola outbreak, and then, in quick succession, the 2020 Covid pandemic, the 2021 Texas power grid outage, and Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine. Sources from both Patriot companies told me that presidential elections also routinely goose sales. One explained that after Donald Trump was reelected in 2024, “we knew that sales were going to plummet, and they did.” He later muses, “I’m genuinely confused why the Left doesn’t start getting prepared.”
paragraphs.zh: 原子毁灭的威胁曾是婴儿潮一代“预备者”最具催化作用的恐惧来源，而许多更年轻的一代则把目光投向了911事件。现代“预备文化”中的其他重大事件还包括卡特里娜飓风、埃博拉疫情，接着又在短时间内接连发生：2020年的新冠疫情、2021年德州电网故障，以及俄罗斯于2022年入侵乌克兰。来自两家 Patriot 公司的人士都告诉我，总统选举也会定期推高销售。一位解释说，在唐纳德·特朗普于2024年连任后，“我们知道销售将会暴跌，而确实如此。”他后来又感叹道：“我真的很困惑，为什么左翼还不开始做准备。”

paragraphs.en: When I entered Stapleton’s home on the morning of September 10, all was normal. Thirty minutes later, though, news broke that right-wing activist Charlie Kirk had been gunned down at Utah Valley University, just nine miles south of American Fork. By the time I descended into the Stapleton family’s subterranean bunker, Kirk’s killer was still on the loose.
paragraphs.zh: 9月10日早上，当我走进 Stapleton 的住所时，一切都很正常。可是在三十分钟后，新闻突然传来：右翼活动人士 Charlie Kirk 在犹他谷大学遭遇枪击身亡，地点在 American Fork 南边仅九英里处。等我走到 Stapleton 一家的地下掩体时，Kirk 的凶手仍然在逃。

paragraphs.en: A few hours later, in front of the hospital where Kirk’s body was being held, I witnessed a vigil of grieving Americans. One was Kelsie Gruenewald, a member of the Latter-day Saints wearing a red, white, and blue T-shirt and holding an American flag. As it turns out, she owns various shelf-stable food products from 4Patriots. In a later interview, Gruenewald tells me that her prepping instincts have hardened in response to various forces, including the day of March 18, 2020, when a 5.7 magnitude earthquake shook Salt Lake City just as Covid-19 was shutting the entire country down. She was again shocked when her abundantly safe, generally moderate home state was struck by political violence. “Evil,” she said, “can creep up anywhere.”
paragraphs.zh: 几个小时后，我在科克遗体被安置的医院门前，看到一群美国人守灵悼念。其中有一位是科尔·格鲁内瓦尔德（Kelsie Gruenewald），她是耶稣基督后期圣徒教会的成员，身穿红白蓝色T恤，手里举着美国国旗。结果我发现，她拥有许多来自4Patriots的常温储存食品。后来在一次采访中，格鲁内瓦尔德告诉我，她的储备意识在多种因素的影响下越来越强。她提到2020年3月18日那一天，5.7级地震在盐湖城发生之际，正好与新冠疫情使全美停摆的时刻重合。如今，即便她原本被认为安全、政治上相对温和的家乡也被政治暴力波及，她再次震惊了。“邪恶，”她说，“可以在任何地方悄悄滋生。”

paragraphs.en: Once Stapleton has awkwardly tested the generator, he calls over to the 4Patriots warehouse for a delivery of food and water supplies. While waiting, I get lunch nearby at the One Man Band Diner. The man running the register is pale and fixated on the television. His daughter was at Kirk’s speech, though by the time of my arrival, she has thankfully been accounted for. I eat next to a deeply tanned carpenter. He tells me he’s had plenty of clients ask him to build a bunker but that, generally, it’s more trouble than it’s worth. “It’s a secretive thing, but you still have to get it permitted,” he explains with a shrug.
paragraphs.zh: 史塔普尔顿拧了拧尴尬地试过发电机后，便打电话给4Patriots仓库，订购食品和水资用品。等待期间，我在附近的一家One Man Band餐馆吃了午饭。收银台的男人脸色苍白，目光几乎黏在电视上。他的女儿出席了科克的追悼演讲，不过当我到达时，她已经无虞地被确认安全。我坐在一位皮肤晒得很黑的木工旁边，他告诉我，许多人都找他要建地堡，但总体来说，这通常比它带来的好处要麻烦得多。“这事儿很隐秘，但还是要去办许可，”他耸肩解释道。

paragraphs.en: The bunker has become the most conspicuous symbol of tinfoil prepping, a mindset nurtured by cynical prepping figures like Chris Turpin, the CEO of the Be Prepared Expo, located in Farmington, Utah. He told one reporter a couple years back that “preparedness helps you from eating your neighbor.” One lapsed Mormon working at a Utah prepping company, who requested anonymity to speak candidly about the industry, is conflicted over how his firm, and others, capitalize on fear. He says that some people “catch an extreme streak” and “go real wild with it,” then mentions a friend whose house not only holds a massive stockpile of food but is also outfitted with trap doors “filled with ammo and guns and precious metals.”
paragraphs.zh: 地堡已成为“铝箔帽式”备灾思维最明显的象征，这种心态由那些玩世不恭的备灾人物不断培养，比如位于犹他州Farmington的Be Prepared Expo首席执行官克里斯·特平（Chris Turpin）。几年前他告诉一位记者，“备灾可以让你不至于去吃你的邻居”。一位在犹他州某家备灾公司工作的脱退摩门教徒，在要求匿名以便坦率谈论这个行业时，表达了对自己公司以及其他公司借恐惧牟利的矛盾心理。他说有人会“陷入极端思维”，然后“把事情搞到失控”，并提到一位朋友的房子，不仅存放了海量食物，还装配了带有陷阱门的密室，里面“堆满了弹药、枪支和贵金属”。

paragraphs.en: After lunch, it’s time to cook up and photograph some food back at Stapleton’s childhood home. It features the staples of Mormon self-reliance: a lush garden, apple trees, chickens, and a basement and bunker stocked with enough food to, in my layman’s estimation, last a lifetime. Stapleton is 4Patriots’ power guy, but still, he gamely navigates the stove while earnestly plugging the product. “Were talking actual recipes, meals,” he explains cheerfully, not simply “mashed potatoes and beans.”
paragraphs.zh: 午饭后，我们回到斯塔普尔顿的童年故居做饭并拍摄食物。那儿展现了摩门教自给自足的典型要素：一座茂盛的花园、苹果树、鸡群，以及一个地下室和地堡，里面储备着足够的食物，按我这种外行人的估计，足以支撑一生。斯塔普尔顿是4Patriots的“主力带货”人物，但他仍然奋力操作炉灶的同时，认真地给产品做推广。“我们说的是实在的食谱、实际的餐食，”他轻松地解释道，而不只是“土豆泥和豆子”那种模板化内容。

paragraphs.en: When our recipe for chocolate pudding demands dairy, we dip into the bunker for dehydrated milk. There, amid 10-foot shelves of beans, corn, pineapple, and other essentials, I spot a 32-gallon can of chocolate milk powder called Morning Moo. I comment incredulously about the incalculable volume of pure happiness that powder must produce. “You never know, man,” Stapleton responds with a cheeky smile. The pudding is serviceable, but the macaroni and cheese is a bit chalky. The pasta is hard, though it’s quite possible we didn’t boil it long enough.
paragraphs.zh: 当我们的巧克力布丁食谱需要奶制品时，我们会从地堡里拿出脱水牛奶。那里有十英尺高的货架，摆着豆类、玉米、菠萝等必需品。我看到一罐32加仑的可可奶粉，品牌名叫Morning Moo。我惊叹地说这罐粉竟然能制造出无法估量的纯粹快乐。“你也没法说得准啊，伙计，”斯泰普尔顿坏笑着回应。布丁还能算合格，但奶酪通心粉有点像粉笔味。意大利面有点硬，不过也很可能是我们没把它煮够时间。

paragraphs.en: The next day, two Latter-day Saints in the employ of My Patriot Supply take me on a tour of their warehouse. Both have blond hair and goatees. The two strenuously emphasize the quality and value of their products over certain unnamed competitors. “A lot of companies will say ‘Look how many servings, look at the weight,’ but they don’t tell you how many calories,” Jared Arvidson, the company’s sales director, explains. “We build pretty much all of our kits 2,000 calories and up. Because you want to be able to live on the food, not just survive.” (4Patriots later tells me it includes caloric information on its food products and also that it harbors no ill will toward its major competitor. My Patriot Supply, for its part, didn’t respond to emailed questions or requests for comment, sent after the tour.)
paragraphs.zh: 第二天，My Patriot Supply的两名耶和华见证人（Latter-day Saints）在职员工带我参观了他们的仓库。两人都留着金发和山羊胡。他们都强调自己的产品在品质和性价比上优于一些未点名的竞争对手。公司销售总监贾里德·阿维森（Jared Arvidson）解释说：“很多公司会说‘看看有多少份量，看这个重量’，但它们不会告诉你有多少卡路里。”他补充道：“我们几乎所有套装都做到了每份至少2,000卡路里。因为你需要的是能靠这些食物活下来，而不仅仅是勉强度过。”（后来，4Patriots告诉我，它的食品上会标注热量信息，并且也表示对主要竞争对手并无恶意。至于My Patriot Supply，在参观后给他们发去邮件提问和评论请求，未予回应。）

paragraphs.en: As we walk the echoey warehouse, Seth McCausland, SVP of manufacturing and operations, also points to the durability, reseal-ability, and UV protective properties of the company’s food pouches, which, he notes, “could be buried under rubble at some point, or they could go through extreme heat or get buried in water from flooding.”
paragraphs.zh: 我们在回声很响的仓库里行走时，制造与运营高管塞思·麦考兹兰（Seth McCausland）还强调了公司食物袋的耐久性、可重复密封性以及防紫外线特性。他指出，“这些袋子在某个时候可能会被埋在废墟下，也可能经受极端高温，或者在洪水中被水淹没。”

paragraphs.en: I am offered a sample of one of their higher-quality food products: a Tuscan sun-dried tomato and sausage pasta. It’s delicious. This, I’m told, is thanks to My Patriot Supply’s in-house food scientist, who they say previously helped invent Subway’s famous raspberry cheesecake cookies. He now formulates nutritionally effective end-of-world meals, in part by boosting them with a proprietary vitamin and mineral blend.
paragraphs.zh: 有人给我尝了一款他们更高端的食品：托斯卡纳风味日晒番茄香肠意大利面。味道很棒。有人告诉我，这得益于My Patriot Supply内部的食品科学家，他们说这位科学家过去曾参与发明赛百味（Subway）著名的覆盆子奶酪曲奇。他现在通过加入一种专有的维生素和矿物质复配物，在一定程度上把这些“末日”餐食调配成更营养有效的版本。

paragraphs.en: The staff at My Patriot Supply contend that they avoid fearmongering, a point the company emphasizes in a YouTube video showing the Baler stand-in cowering on the ground while warning about murder hornets. Near the end of the tour, however, I see, amid stacks of inventory, a series of books by a doomsaying prepper named Teddy Daniels. His titles include Devil’s Dollar: How to Survive the Final War on Christians and Final Famine: How to Keep Your Family Fat and Happy While the World Starves. Arvidson quickly clarifies that the firm no longer sells these products—and indeed they’re not for sale online—then shuffles us off to another section. Later, though, I find social media content from the company verging on conspiratorial, including a chart it shared on X depicting all the Chinese-owned farmland next to military bases. In later research, I also found a clip of Glenn Beck plugging the firm’s heirloom seed packets.
paragraphs.zh: My Patriot Supply 的员工表示他们避免制造恐慌，公司的 YouTube 视频中也强调了这一点：视频里 Baler 的替代人蜷缩在地上，边说边警告“杀人蜂”问题。可在参观快结束时，我却在一叠库存货品中看到一系列由一位悲观预言式备灾者 Teddy Daniels 出版的书。书名包括《恶魔之钞：如何在针对基督徒的末日战争中生存》和《终末饥荒：在世界挨饿时如何让你的家人又饱又开心》。Arvidson 很快澄清说公司已经不再销售这些产品——它们也确实没有在网上出售——然后就把我们带到另一段区域。但是后来我在该公司社交媒体上又看到近乎阴谋论的内容，包括它在 X 上分享的一张图，展示了所有中国所有的农田与军事基地相邻的情况。在后续研究中，我还发现了 Glenn Beck 推介该公司传家宝种子包的一个视频片段。

paragraphs.en: In 2022, the two companies leveled dozens of claims and counterclaims against each other before the National Advertising Division, an independent non-profit with links to the Better Business Bureau, regarding everything from the integrity of their online reviews to the quality of their products. In the end, the division dinged both companies on similar fronts, recommending, for instance, that 4Patriots avoid conveying to consumers that its survival food kits “provide consumers with the abundance of food depicted in the advertising for the full claimed duration,” while urging My Patriot Supply to stop indicating that its food kits included fresh fruit. Both companies were also criticized for how they sought to juice the review landscape, either by withholding negative feedback, in the case of My Patriot Supply, or, for 4Patriots, through failing to properly disclose its financial endorsements of certain product review videos.
paragraphs.zh: 2022 年，这两家公司在国家广告审议局前就数十项彼此的指控和反指控展开交锋。该机构是一个与全美商业局（BBB）有联系的独立非营利组织。争议内容从线上评价的可信度到产品质量无所不包。最终，该机构在多项问题上都对两家公司提出了批评。比如，它建议 4Patriots 避免向消费者暗示其生存食品套装“可在广告中宣称的持续时长内，为消费者提供充足的食物”；同时也要求 My Patriot Supply 停止暗示其食品套装内含新鲜水果。两家公司还被指在“操控”评论环境方面做法不当：My Patriot Supply 被批评为压制负面反馈，而 4Patriots 则被指出没有在某些产品评测视频中正确披露其经济代言关系。

paragraphs.en: Like siblings born in too-quick succession, My Patriot Supply and 4Patriots seem to be squabbling over their similarities as much as their differences. They both exist to sell a sense of security in a rapidly destabilizing world. The threats are certainly real, even if the exact solution—a new generator, a shelf stable meal—might not always be so satisfying.
paragraphs.zh: 像是连生过太快的兄妹一样，My Patriot Supply 和 4Patriots 似乎为彼此的相似点争吵得不亚于真正的分歧点。它们共同存在的目的，都是在一个快速动荡的世界里出售一种安全感。威胁当然确实存在，尽管最终方案——一台新发电机、一份常温耐储食物——未必总能带来真正令人满足的安全感。

paragraphs.en: Update: 1/8/2025, 10:10 AM EDT: WIRED updated the relationship between the National Advertising Division and the Better Business Bureau.
paragraphs.zh: 更新：2025 年 1 月 8 日，东部夏令时上午 10:10：WIRED 更新了国家广告审议局与全美商业局之间的关系。
